title: Implement SHA agent enrollment and posture reporting
description: Why now: SHA already has the first control-plane slice, but SummitFlow still needs an execution-ready task package for the agent-facing reporting boundary. This task defines and verifies the first safe agent increment: Windows/Linux enrollment, heartbeat, bounded read-only posture reporting, deterministic installer artifacts, endpoint inventory/detail visibility, and machine-readable shared contracts. It intentionally stops before typed remediation execution and rollback mutation workflows.
done_when: `POST /api/endpoints/enroll` accepts `{agent_fingerprint, hostname, platform, platform_version?, agent_version, tenant_id?, site_id?}` and returns the full endpoint object with `endpoint_id`, normalized fingerprint, explicit nullable keys, `status`, `last_seen_at`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`. Same normalized fingerprint re-enrollment preserves the record, omitted optional fields preserve stored values, explicit null clears them, and same-fingerprint cross-platform reuse returns HTTP 409.

`POST /api/endpoints/{endpoint_id}/heartbeat` accepts `{agent_version, platform_version?, platform_profile, connectivity_status, declared_capabilities[], execution_hooks}` where capability values are trim-normalized, validated against the pinned verb set, de-duplicated by normalized value, and stored in sorted order; `execution_hooks` is required and must include `captures_rollback_artifacts`, `reports_execution_results`, and `supports_dry_run` booleans.

`POST /api/posture-snapshots` accepts `{endpoint_id, observed_at, platform_profile, results[]}` where each result contains `control_key`, `status`, `current_value?`, `recommended_value?`, `severity?`, `evidence_summary`, and `reboot_required`. `observed_at` normalizes to UTC `Z`, empty results are rejected, unknown endpoints return HTTP 404, and duplicate logical control keys inside one snapshot return HTTP 422.

`GET /api/endpoints` and `GET /api/endpoints/{endpoint_id}` expose the latest posture snapshot ordered by `observed_at DESC, snapshot_id DESC`; latest results come from that snapshot ordered by `control_key ASC`, and when no snapshot exists the summary is null while detail results are an empty list.

SHA installer profiles generate deterministic Linux shell and Windows PowerShell artifacts through `GET /api/installer-profiles/{profile_id}/artifact`, and repeated downloads for the same profile are byte-identical with matching `X-SHA-Artifact-Sha256` hashes and stable `Content-Disposition` filenames.

The bounded reporters embed only profile/config values needed for enroll -> heartbeat -> posture (`profile_id`, `profile_name`, `platform`, `channel`, `control_plane_url`, `policy_mode`, `tenant_id`, `site_id`, `agent_version`, `platform_profile`, `capabilities`, `execution_hooks`) and compute fingerprints as SHA-256 hex over `<platform>|<profile_id>|<machine-identity>`.

The Next.js operator UI exposes working same-origin surfaces for home, fleet, endpoint detail, and installers, while the existing controls/approvals shell remains buildable without backend fetches at build time.

`schemas/generated/` contains checked-in exports produced by `model.model_json_schema(ref_template='#/$defs/{model}')` for the public request/response contracts, written with sorted keys and a trailing newline, and `schemas/generated/manifest.json` is fixed to `{schema_version, source_module, files[{filename, model}]}`.
Verification proves `uv run pytest` in `backend/`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and `dt --check` from the repo root.
objective: Lock the first SHA agent-facing reporting slice into an execution-ready, SummitFlow-tracked contract so future autonomous work can safely extend it without drifting across backend, frontend, installer, and agent boundaries.
spirit_anti: Do not implement arbitrary shell access, broad remote administration, write-capable remediation executors, certificate-heavy mutual-trust infrastructure, or long-lived rollback workflows in this task. Keep the slice read-mostly, bounded, and verifiable.
constraints: This slice covers Windows and Linux only. macOS remains out of scope.
The host does not require a Go toolchain for this increment; generated reporters and repo-local contracts are the authoritative agent boundary for now.

Enrollment identity is server-owned. The backend issues `endpoint_id`, server-side uniqueness is the normalized fingerprint string only, and fingerprint normalization is trim + lowercase. The packaged reporters intentionally make that fingerprint equal to SHA-256 hex of `<platform>|<profile_id>|<machine-identity>` so one host may produce distinct endpoint identities per installer profile.

If the same normalized fingerprint re-enrolls with the same platform, the existing endpoint record is updated in place. If the same normalized fingerprint arrives with a different `platform`, the request returns HTTP 409 and must not mutate the existing record.

`connectivity_status` is a pinned enum with values `online|degraded`. `platform_profile` is a required trimmed opaque string whose case is preserved after trim; this task does not upgrade it into a server-owned enum.

Heartbeat scope is bounded to agent version, platform version/profile, connectivity status, declared capability verbs, and execution-hook booleans. In this slice, execution hooks are observational metadata only: `captures_rollback_artifacts`, `reports_execution_results`, and `supports_dry_run`. The heartbeat request requires all three booleans; omitted `platform_version` preserves the prior value, explicit null clears it, and inventory/detail responses return null for execution hooks until the first heartbeat stores them.

Capability verbs must stay inside the hardening boundary: `enroll`, `heartbeat`, `collect_posture_snapshot`, `inspect_control`, `apply_control`, `rollback_control`, `collect_security_context`, `collect_remediation_evidence`, and `request_elevated_troubleshooting`. Normalization is trim + enum validation, duplicate normalized values are rejected, and the stored/displayed order is stable sorted order.

Posture result status is limited to `pass`, `fail`, `warn`, `error`, and `not_applicable`. `control_key` duplicate detection is trim + lowercase within one snapshot; stored `control_key` preserves trimmed original casing while `control_key_normalized` holds the lowered comparison key.

Before the first heartbeat, endpoint inventory/detail responses expose `connectivity_status=null`, `declared_capabilities=[]`, and `execution_hooks=null`. Before the first posture snapshot, `latest_posture_summary=null` and endpoint detail returns `latest_results=[]`.

Generated reporters remain read-only and bounded. Linux posture checks are limited to firewall service state, SSH password-auth configuration, root-password lock state, and automatic update enablement. Windows posture checks are limited to firewall profile state, Defender real-time protection, BitLocker system-drive protection, and Secure Boot state.
No arbitrary shell execution, filesystem browsing, or generic remote command hooks may appear anywhere in the installer artifacts, backend contracts, or frontend UX for this task.

Heartbeat/reporting cadence is the packaged 15 minute timer/task path used by the generated artifacts. Offline buffering, retry/backoff beyond immediate packaged behavior, and reconnect queues are explicitly deferred.

Installer artifact determinism means exact bytes for repeated downloads of the same profile under the pinned dependency/runtime set, not merely logical equivalence after stripping timestamps or line endings.

Shared contract exports under `schemas/generated/` must be derived from the backend Pydantic models rather than handwritten duplicates. Approval request/grant schemas remain in the bundle because they are already part of the public same-origin contract surface, but this task does not require new approval behavior beyond keeping exports synchronized.

`schemas/generated/manifest.json` follows the exporter tuple order and is fixed to `schema_version=1`, `source_module='app.schemas.contracts'`, and `files[]` entries containing only `{filename, model}` in deterministic order.

Future typed remediation and rollback mutation workflows are a follow-up task; this task may describe those boundaries but must not ship write-capable execution beyond the bounded reporting metadata already present.
testing_strategy: Keep focused backend tests for enrollment idempotency, optional-field clearing, cross-platform fingerprint conflicts, heartbeat capability normalization and ordering, endpoint inventory/detail latest-posture selection, installer artifact byte-for-byte determinism, approval/reporting contract validation, and persistence round-trips.
Add a regression test that proves the shared `schemas/generated/` exports exist and stay synchronized with `backend/app/schemas/contracts.py`, including `schemas/generated/manifest.json` shape.
Run `uv run pytest` from `backend/`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and `dt --check` from the repo root.
Keep the Linux VM100/bootstrap evidence in task logs or repo docs as supporting operational proof, but do not make historical runtime evidence the only closeout gate.

risks: If execution hooks are described too loosely, future work can accidentally reintroduce arbitrary-shell semantics through metadata drift.
If shared schema exports are not kept in sync with the backend Pydantic models, future agent or SHAna integrations can silently drift from the live API.
If the task bundle keeps remediation/rollback implementation in scope, critique and closeout will continue to fail because the current slice is intentionally narrower and safer.

subtasks.description: Freeze the reporting-side enrollment, heartbeat, inventory/detail, and posture contracts before any future agent execution work extends them.

steps.description: Keep enrollment idempotent for normalized fingerprints and reject cross-platform reuse with HTTP 409.

spec.detail: This task owns only the reporting identity boundary: server-generated `endpoint_id`, fingerprint normalization as trim + lowercase, reporter fingerprints equal to SHA-256 hex of `<platform>|<profile_id>|<machine-identity>`, preserved records on same-platform re-enroll, and explicit null-vs-omitted semantics for `platform_version`, `tenant_id`, and `site_id`.

steps.description: Keep heartbeat payloads typed and bounded to connectivity plus declared capability metadata.

spec.detail: Do not add free-form command channels. Heartbeats accept `agent_version`, `platform_version?`, `platform_profile`, `connectivity_status`, `declared_capabilities[]`, and required `execution_hooks`. Capability values are normalized against the pinned enum, duplicate normalized values are rejected, stored/displayed order is sorted, and `execution_hooks` must always carry the three named booleans.

steps.description: Keep posture reporting read-only and normalized across platforms.

spec.detail: The reporting route accepts typed results with status enum `pass|fail|warn|error|not_applicable`, rejects duplicate logical control keys inside one snapshot with HTTP 422, returns HTTP 404 for unknown endpoints, and surfaces latest summary/results through inventory/detail using snapshot order `observed_at DESC, snapshot_id DESC` plus result order `control_key ASC`.

subtasks.description: Make installer artifacts and bundled reporters the concrete cross-platform agent boundary for this first increment.

steps.description: Generate deterministic Linux and Windows installer artifacts per profile.

spec.detail: Linux artifacts install `/opt/sha/reporter.py`, `/etc/sha/reporter-config.json`, and a systemd timer/service. Windows artifacts install `C:\ProgramData\SHA\reporter.ps1`, `C:\ProgramData\SHA\reporter-config.json`, and a recurring scheduled task. Repeated downloads for the same profile must be exact-byte identical and produce matching `X-SHA-Artifact-Sha256` hashes plus stable filename slugs in `Content-Disposition`.

steps.description: Constrain the reporter to enrollment, heartbeat, and bounded posture upload only.

spec.detail: The packaged reporters may inspect the pinned Linux/Windows posture families in this task, but they must not expose arbitrary shell or filesystem access. The embedded config surface is limited to `profile_id`, `profile_name`, `platform`, `channel`, `control_plane_url`, `policy_mode`, `tenant_id`, `site_id`, `agent_version`, `platform_profile`, `capabilities`, and `execution_hooks`.

subtasks.description: Expose the reporting slice through the same-origin operator workspace.

steps.description: Keep fleet, endpoint detail, controls, approvals, and installers pages buildable without backend fetches at build time.

spec.detail: The frontend must render operator-ready same-origin surfaces, route to live endpoint details when the backend is reachable, and preserve a stable static build path. Home, fleet, endpoint detail, and installers are the reporting-slice surfaces; controls and approvals only need to remain buildable and visually consistent with the same shell.

steps.description: Show normalized reporting data rather than placeholder-only copy.

spec.detail: Endpoint detail and fleet views should surface latest posture summaries, connectivity state, declared capabilities, execution-hook null-vs-present behavior, and installer/reporting context from the bounded API models. When no posture snapshot exists yet, the UI must tolerate a null summary and empty detail results without backend-only build dependencies.

subtasks.description: Check in machine-readable shared schemas for the reporting boundary.

steps.description: Export JSON Schemas from the backend Pydantic models into `schemas/generated/`.

spec.detail: The exporter must be deterministic, checked into the repo, and cover the public request/response contracts used by the reporting slice so future agent or SHAna work can consume one authoritative bundle. The export format is exactly `model.model_json_schema(ref_template='#/$defs/{model}')` serialized with `json.dumps(indent=2, sort_keys=True)` plus a trailing newline.

steps.description: Verify the generated schema bundle stays synchronized with the backend contracts.

spec.detail: Closeout requires a regression test that fails when `schemas/generated/` drifts from `backend/app/schemas/contracts.py`. `schemas/generated/manifest.json` is part of that contract and must contain `schema_version`, `source_module`, and `files[{filename, model}]`.

subtasks.description: Prove the reporting slice is stable and safe to extend.

steps.description: Run backend, frontend, and managed repo checks.

spec.detail: Mandatory closeout commands are `uv run pytest` in `backend/`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and `dt --check`.

steps.description: Keep live evidence of the packaged Linux reporting path.

spec.detail: The repo/task context should preserve that a generated Linux bootstrap artifact already completed enroll -> heartbeat -> posture reporting against the running SHA control plane, even though broader offline buffering or remediation execution remains out of scope for this task.
